title: Sketches Object
description: Provides access to the sketches within a design and provides methods to create new sketches.

methods.name: add
methods.description: Creates a new sketch on the specified planar entity.

methods.name: addToBaseOrFormFeature
methods.description: Creates a parametric sketch that is associated with a base feature.   Because of a current limitation, if you want to create a sketch associated with a base feature, you must first call the edit method of the base feature, use this method to create the sketch, and then call the finishEdit method of the base feature. The base feature must be in an "edit" state to be able to add any additional items to it.

methods.name: addWithoutEdges
methods.description: Creates a new sketch on the specified planar entity. If a BRepFace is provided, the edges of the face are not projected into the sketch so the result of creating a new sketch with this method will always be a new empty sketch.

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

methods.name: item
methods.description: Function that returns the specified sketch using an index into the collection.

methods.name: itemByName
methods.description: Returns the sketch with the specified name.

properties.name: count
properties.description: Returns the number of sketches in a component

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

related_samples.name: API Sample that demonstrates creating sketch lines in various ways.
related_samples.description: Demonstrates several ways to create sketch lines, including as the result of creating a rectangle.

related_samples.name: Loft Feature API Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates creating a new loft feature.

related_samples.name: Patch Feature API Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates creating a new patch feature.

add.title: Sketches.add Method
add.description: Creates a new sketch on the specified planar entity.

syntax.python:
# Uses no optional arguments.
returnValue = sketches_var.add(planarEntity)

# Uses optional arguments.
returnValue = sketches_var.add(planarEntity, occurrenceForCreation)

add.return_description: Returns the newly created Sketch or null if the creation failed.

parameters.name: planarEntity
parameters.description: A construction plane or planar face that defines the sketch plane

parameters.name: occurrenceForCreation
parameters.description: A creation occurrence is needed if the planarEntity is in another component AND the sketch is not in the root component. The occurrenceForCreation is analogous to the active occurrence in the UI.   This is an optional argument whose default value is null.

related_samples.name: API Sample that demonstrates creating sketch lines in various ways.
related_samples.description: Demonstrates several ways to create sketch lines, including as the result of creating a rectangle.

related_samples.name: Loft Feature API Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates creating a new loft feature.

related_samples.name: Patch Feature API Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates creating a new patch feature.

related_samples.name: Simple Extrusion Sample
related_samples.description: Creates a new extrusion feature, resulting in a new component.

related_samples.name: Simple Revolve Feature Sample
related_samples.description: Creates a new revolve feature, resulting in a new component.

addToBaseOrFormFeature.title: Sketches.addToBaseOrFormFeature Method
addToBaseOrFormFeature.description: Creates a parametric sketch that is associated with a base feature. Because of a current limitation, if you want to create a sketch associated with a base feature, you must first call the edit method of the base feature, use this method to create the sketch, and then call the finishEdit method of the base feature. The base feature must be in an "edit" state to be able to add any additional items to it.
addToBaseOrFormFeature.return_description: Returns the newly created Sketch or null if the creation failed.

parameters.name: planarEntity
parameters.description: A construction plane or planar face that defines the sketch plane.

parameters.name: targetBaseOrFormFeature
parameters.description: The existing base feature that you want to associate this sketch with.

parameters.name: includeFaceEdges
parameters.description: When a BrepFace is used as the planarEntity argument, this defines if the edges of the face should be included in the sketch.

addWithoutEdges.title: Sketches.addWithoutEdges Method
addWithoutEdges.description: Creates a new sketch on the specified planar entity. If a BRepFace is provided, the edges of the face are not projected into the sketch so the result of creating a new sketch with this method will always be a new empty sketch.

syntax.python:
# Uses no optional arguments.
returnValue = sketches_var.addWithoutEdges(planarEntity)

# Uses optional arguments.
returnValue = sketches_var.addWithoutEdges(planarEntity, occurrenceForCreation)

addWithoutEdges.return_description: Returns the newly created Sketch or null if the creation failed.

parameters.name: planarEntity
parameters.description: A construction plane or planar face that defines the sketch plane.

parameters.name: occurrenceForCreation
parameters.description: A creation occurrence is needed if the planarEntity is in another component AND the sketch is not in the root component. The occurrenceForCreation is analogous to the active occurrence in the UI.   This is an optional argument whose default value is null.

classType.title: Sketches.classType Method
classType.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().
classType.return_description: Returns a string indicating the type of the object.

item.title: Sketches.item Method
item.description: Function that returns the specified sketch using an index into the collection.
item.return_description: Returns the specified item or null if an invalid index was specified.

parameters.name: index
parameters.description: The index of the item within the collection to return. The first item in the collection has an index of 0.

itemByName.title: Sketches.itemByName Method
itemByName.description: Returns the sketch with the specified name.
itemByName.return_description: Returns the sketch or null if there isn't a sketch with that name.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The name of the sketch as seen in the browser and the timeline.

count.title: Sketches.count Property
count.description: Returns the number of sketches in a component

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = sketches_var.count

related_samples.name: Delete Empty Components
related_samples.description: Deletes empty components from the active design.

isValid.title: Sketches.isValid Property
isValid.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = sketches_var.isValid

objectType.title: Sketches.objectType Property
objectType.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object. It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = sketches_var.objectType
